title: eugenia montalbanica
intro: Eugenia montalbanica, also known as the Philippine mountain apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a shrub that grows up to 3 meters in height and has white flowers that produce small, edible fruits. Eugenia montalbanica has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, and it can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion for its medicinal uses.
